title: Xtra Crispy Korean Sushi Rice Jeon
description: A crispy and adventurous Korean dish featuring sushi rice, infused with oil and wrapped in pandan leaves, this jeon is a unique twist on traditional Gangwon cuisine, and is low in sodium.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the cooled sushi rice, infuse oil, yeast, salt, and black pepper, and mix well.
Add the chopped green onions, toasted sesame seeds, and beaten egg to the bowl, and mix until a sticky dough forms.
Divide the dough into 12 equal pieces, and shape each piece into a small patty.
Wrap each patty in a fresh pandan leaf, and press the edges to seal.
Dip each wrapped patty in the all purpose flour, shaking off any excess.
Heat about 1/2 inch of infuse oil in a large skillet over medium heat, and fry the wrapped patties until crispy and golden brown, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Drain the fried jeon on paper towels, and serve hot.
Repeat the frying process with the remaining patties, and serve immediately.
